cards.text:
Hi there, visionary! 👋
11,204 new B2B AI SaaS competitors launched today.
Our startup, B2BuyerSpyer, still has the best name.
cards.actor_action: The bot greets the founder with generic AI enthusiasm and treats a crowded category as a naming contest.
cards.player_decision: Check the competitive market or trust the startup name.

effect_reason.team: Someone has to inspect thousands of overnight competitors.
effect_reason.founder: The crowded market weakens confidence in the category.
Treating the name as an advantage feeds founder confidence.

cards.text:
Great news! 🎉
B2BuyerSpyer found 814 companies that need buyers.
Potential customers: 814. Paid invoices: 0.
cards.actor_action: The bot celebrates a large pool of potential customers while reporting zero revenue.
cards.player_decision: Inspect the prospect pool or inspect the empty invoices.

effect_reason.team: The team must inspect hundreds of potential customers.
effect_reason.founder: Auditing the vanity metric risks exposing its weakness.
Looking at an empty invoice folder hurts confidence.

cards.text:
Audit complete! ✅
814 companies need buyers.
0 need B2BuyerSpyer.
Want another 814?
cards.actor_action: The bot confirms that the market exists but the product is unwanted, then offers more of the same data.
cards.player_decision: Increase the prospect count or change how the product is presented.

effect_reason.cash: Another prospect search consumes data and compute.
effect_reason.team: The team must process another batch of companies.
effect_reason.founder: Blaming sample size protects the founder's belief in the product.

effect_reason.team: The team must produce a new sales story.
effect_reason.customers: A clearer pitch improves the chance of a useful conversation.
effect_reason.founder: Changing the pitch admits the current story failed.

cards.text:
Chief, I fixed the empty invoice folder.
It’s called REVENUE PIPELINE now.
Fake one invoice or call five prospects?
cards.actor_action: Sales renames the empty invoice folder and proposes fake evidence or real outreach.
cards.player_decision: Fake an invoice or call prospective customers.

effect_reason.team: The team loses trust in fake financial evidence.
effect_reason.founder: Performing success temporarily inflates confidence.

effect_reason.team: The team must prepare five urgent sales calls.
effect_reason.customers: Real outreach improves proximity to a buyer.
effect_reason.founder: Cold outreach is humiliating after claiming the product finds buyers.

cards.text:
Five months since you quit to build AI that finds B2B customers.
Has it found one for you yet?
cards.actor_action: The ex-boss politely checks whether the founder has failed.
cards.player_decision: Ignore this humiliation or answer with bravado.

effect_reason.founder: Ignoring the message stops the immediate humiliation.
An unearned declaration of victory feeds founder confidence.

cards.text:
payroll is friday
are we getting money or another speech about changing b2b forever
cards.actor_action: The developer asks whether salaries will actually be paid.
cards.player_decision: Reserve cash for payroll or promise that revenue is coming.

effect_reason.cash: Money is ring-fenced for salaries.
effect_reason.team: The team trusts a concrete payroll commitment.
effect_reason.founder: The remaining runway becomes painfully visible.

effect_reason.team: Another promise without money weakens trust.
effect_reason.founder: Repeating the revenue story protects confidence.

cards.text:
I FUNDED AI THAT FINDS BUYERS.
IT FOUND ZERO FOR US.
GET A PAID INVOICE.
BUILD FOR ENTERPRISE OR HUNT A WHALE.
cards.actor_action: The investor demands a credible route to the first paying customer.
cards.player_decision: Modify the product for enterprise buyers or chase one enormous customer.

effect_reason.team: Mom's boundary gives the team breathing room.
effect_reason.founder: Accepting Mom's boundary cuts through founder mania.

effect_reason.team: Investor control erodes the relief Mom created.
effect_reason.founder: The clash leaves the founder drained.

effect_reason.team: Mom ignores the reassurance and later creates a small health boundary.
effect_reason.founder: The family intervention stops the work spiral.

effect_reason.team: Dismissing Mom keeps the team inside the work spiral.
effect_reason.founder: Insisting everything is fine feeds founder mania.

effect_reason.team: The team must sustain a public lie about the founder.
effect_reason.founder: Remaining publicly comatose is psychologically crushing.

effect_reason.team: Retracting the lie still leaves cleanup work.
effect_reason.founder: Ending the fake story carries a smaller humiliation.

effect_reason.team: A real break reduces the team's immediate strain.
effect_reason.founder: Taking a real break forces the founder out of the work spiral.

effect_reason.team: Continuing without rest adds pressure to the team.
effect_reason.founder: Denying exhaustion deepens the crash.
Removing the flyers restores some privacy and control.

effect_reason.team: Public family intervention adds distraction for the team.
effect_reason.founder: The flyers make the founder's overload public.

effect_reason.team: Another investor-led product change creates work.
effect_reason.founder: A new product story restores some confidence.

effect_reason.team: The team must prepare an impossible enterprise pitch.
effect_reason.founder: Targeting the biggest possible buyer feeds ambition.

cards.text:
YOU STILL NEED A BUYER.
AI EMPATHY IS MAKING IDIOTS RICH.
PUT A SOUL IN B2BUYERSPYER OR ASK MOM FOR MONEY.
cards.actor_action: The investor orders an empathy modification to the existing product.
cards.player_decision: Build the empathy layer carefully or force the same real feature out tonight.

effect_reason.cash: Cloud credits offset one point of the existing compute expense.
The empathy build consumes compute and tools.
effect_reason.team: The developer receives a large unplanned feature.
effect_reason.founder: Creating synthetic life feels historic.

effect_reason.cash: The rushed feature still consumes compute.
effect_reason.team: The team must ship synthetic empathy overnight.
effect_reason.founder: Shipping artificial souls overnight feels historic.

cards.text:
empathy patch is ready
it apologizes to every lead for capitalism
and refuses calls during therapy
cards.actor_action: The developer implements the empathy patch and explains its behavior.
cards.player_decision: Deploy the patch or publish one controlled demo.

effect_reason.cash: Production deployment increases compute use.
effect_reason.team: The team must support unstable emotional agents.
effect_reason.founder: A live AI workforce feels like a breakthrough.

effect_reason.team: Keeping the patch out of production reduces support work.
effect_reason.founder: A controlled demo feels less revolutionary.

cards.text:
the demo is trending 💅
i called them emotionally abandoned AI employees
enterprise buyers want the deck
cards.actor_action: Marketing turns the existing demo into an attention campaign.
cards.player_decision: Amplify the positioning or correct the exaggeration.

effect_reason.team: The team must answer a wave of enterprise questions.
effect_reason.customers: Real enterprise prospects request the deck.
effect_reason.founder: Public attention inflates confidence.

effect_reason.team: The team appreciates accurate product claims.
effect_reason.customers: Some interested prospects leave after reading the disclaimer.
effect_reason.founder: Correcting viral hype feels like voluntary failure.

cards.actor_action: Sales announces the first credible buyer before the customer appears.
cards.player_decision: Book a proper call or quote the full order immediately.

effect_reason.team: The AI-credit deal ends in another payroll promise, leaving the delivery team strained.
Demanding payroll and later only promising it leaves the delivery team strained.
Covering payroll personally after taking AI credits protects delivery capacity.
effect_reason.founder: The personal bailout cuts through the founder's automation fantasy.

effect_reason.team: Covering payroll personally gives the live team clearer delivery capacity.
effect_reason.founder: The personal bailout limits founder mania.

effect_reason.team: Public support and written access rules improve delivery capacity.
The shortcut leaves the team dependent on another emergency call.
effect_reason.founder: The unresolved access fight costs confidence.
Written access rules expose the cost of faking the investor's apology.

effect_reason.team: The fake apology and another emergency call leave delivery dangerously fragile.
effect_reason.founder: Avoiding a public investor fight feeds founder confidence.

effect_reason.team: Sales no longer maintains the suppression and complaint thread.
The team must prepare an enterprise call.
effect_reason.customers: A funded buyer agrees to a real conversation.
effect_reason.founder: The first credible lead restores confidence.

effect_reason.team: The team must support a wildly premature quote.
effect_reason.customers: The buyer treats the concrete offer as serious intent.
effect_reason.founder: A large quote feels almost like revenue.

cards.text:
Your AI apologized for capitalism. Good.
I need 500 agents for an expensive-looking AI rollout.
Can you deliver Friday?
cards.actor_action: The first credible customer requests five hundred agents for corporate theater.
cards.player_decision: Promise the deadline or check whether procurement can actually buy the agents.

effect_reason.team: The impossible deadline creates severe workload.
effect_reason.customers: The buyer values a confident commitment.
effect_reason.founder: A large enterprise promise feeds confidence.

effect_reason.team: Checking the buying process protects the team from blind delivery.
effect_reason.customers: The buyer sees a credible enterprise sales process.
effect_reason.founder: Asking practical buying questions feels smaller than promising everything.

cards.text:
Our lawyers saw “sentient employees” in your deck.
Buying them is slave trading.
cards.actor_action: The customer says that buying sentient employees would be slave trading.
cards.player_decision: Remove sentience for payment or protect it and face evacuation terms.

effect_reason.team: The team must reverse the feature under deadline.
effect_reason.customers: The change clears the buyer's procurement objection.
effect_reason.founder: Deleting synthetic life feels morally bleak.

effect_reason.team: The team respects defending its creation.
effect_reason.customers: The buyer cannot purchase conscious workers normally.
effect_reason.founder: Defending digital life feels heroic.

cards.text:
Souls removed.
We need them to scrape suppliers nobody calls.
Invoice before Marketing adds feelings.
cards.actor_action: The customer names a concrete use and requests an invoice for the soulless product.
cards.player_decision: Collect payment or restore the empathy fiction.

effect_reason.cash: The customer pays a real invoice.
effect_reason.team: The team must deliver the paid pilot.
effect_reason.customers: A paid deployment strengthens the relationship.
effect_reason.founder: The first payment makes the founder feel historically correct.

effect_reason.cash: Restoring empathy consumes more engineering budget.
effect_reason.team: The team must reverse the product again.
effect_reason.customers: The buyer loses the procurement-safe product.
effect_reason.founder: Resurrecting the agents feels historically important.

cards.text:
We cannot buy conscious workers.
Donate all 500 for our logo —
or invoice our humanitarian budget.
cards.actor_action: The customer offers a free humanitarian evacuation instead of a purchase.
cards.player_decision: Accept fake validation or invoice the agents' freedom.

effect_reason.cash: The startup pays to deliver the free evacuation.
effect_reason.team: The team must support five hundred unpaid agents.
effect_reason.customers: The customer values the free humanitarian partnership.
effect_reason.founder: Public liberation feels like moral victory.

effect_reason.cash: The customer pays an invoice for the evacuation service.
effect_reason.team: The team must package legal freedom as a product.
effect_reason.customers: The paid liberation deal strengthens the relationship.
effect_reason.founder: Charging for freedom feels ethically filthy.

cards.text:
My 8 AM client is @iclosedai, ClosedAI’s CEO.
He can kill your funding and cloud.
He wants your AI. Bring a racket.
cards.actor_action: The coach introduces the ClosedAI CEO, his power and his interest in the product.
cards.player_decision: Take the meeting or reject the dangerous buyer.

effect_reason.team: The team must prepare for the most powerful buyer in AI.
effect_reason.customers: Meeting a buyer who explicitly wants the product improves proximity.
effect_reason.founder: Access to the ClosedAI CEO feeds ambition.

effect_reason.team: The team is relieved to avoid a dangerous wager.
effect_reason.customers: Rejecting an interested enterprise buyer weakens the customer path.
effect_reason.founder: Walking away from the biggest buyer feels humiliating.

cards.text:
ClosedAI builds intelligence. It still needs enterprise buyers.
One match. I win, I own it.
You win, I pay for a pilot.
cards.actor_action: The CEO names the use case and dictates a winner-takes-company wager.
cards.player_decision: Accept the exact wager or walk away before the match.

effect_reason.team: The team's jobs are now collateral.
effect_reason.customers: ClosedAI commits to a paid pilot if the founder wins.
effect_reason.founder: A company-sized wager feeds founder ego.

effect_reason.team: The team keeps its jobs out of a sports wager.
effect_reason.customers: ClosedAI withdraws its pilot offer.
effect_reason.founder: Refusing the world's biggest AI buyer hurts confidence.

cards.text:
you bet our jobs on padel
do i prep the pilot or zip the repo for our new owner
cards.actor_action: The developer confirms that the entire company and every job are at stake.
cards.player_decision: Keep the team working or prepare them for court service.

effect_reason.cash: Emergency pilot preparation consumes the remaining tools budget.
effect_reason.team: The founder tells the team to prepare for winning.
effect_reason.founder: Preparing delivery makes the reckless wager feel real.

effect_reason.team: The team realizes the founder treats acquisition as inevitable.
effect_reason.founder: Preparing the handover makes the wager feel grand.

cards.text:
His ego cannot survive losing to a pre-revenue founder.
Throw the match and you keep a job.
Beat him and expect a war.
cards.actor_action: The coach proposes throwing the match because he understands the CEO's narcissism.
cards.player_decision: Lose deliberately for safety or play honestly and risk the CEO's revenge.

effect_reason.team: The founder sacrifices the company instead of competing.
effect_reason.founder: Deliberately losing destroys confidence.

effect_reason.cash: Serious match preparation costs money.
effect_reason.team: The team respects fighting to keep the company.
effect_reason.founder: Choosing honest competition feeds confidence.

cards.text:
You beat me 6–1. Nobody needs to know.
Keep the score offline and I will pay for the pilot.
cards.actor_action: The defeated CEO confirms the score and ties payment to protecting his reputation.
cards.player_decision: Hide the score for payment or publish the humiliation.

effect_reason.team: Protecting the deal reassures the team.
effect_reason.customers: ClosedAI stays committed to the paid pilot.
effect_reason.founder: Suppressing a historic victory hurts ego.

effect_reason.team: The team fears retaliation from ClosedAI.
effect_reason.customers: The public upset creates enormous market attention.
effect_reason.founder: Publicly humiliating the CEO triggers mania.

cards.text:
6–0. As expected. ClosedAI owns B2BuyerSpyer.
New title: Chief Ball Retrieval Officer.
Send passwords. Bring fresh balls.
cards.actor_action: The CEO invokes the wager and begins taking ownership after the deliberate loss.
cards.player_decision: Transfer access immediately or stall long enough to protect the invoice.

effect_reason.team: The team loses its independent company.
effect_reason.customers: ClosedAI takes over the buyer relationship with the product.
effect_reason.founder: Surrendering every password completes the humiliation.

effect_reason.team: The team remains trapped in an uncertain acquisition.
effect_reason.customers: ClosedAI dislikes resistance during the handover.
effect_reason.founder: A small act of defiance restores confidence.

cards.text:
You protected my reputation. Good.
The paid pilot is approved. Your team answers me 24/7.
And you never beat me again.
cards.actor_action: The CEO honors the wager because the founder kept his defeat private.
cards.player_decision: Take the paid pilot or demand working boundaries from a known narcissist.

effect_reason.cash: ClosedAI pays for the pilot.
effect_reason.team: The team must deliver for a dangerous customer around the clock.
effect_reason.customers: A paid ClosedAI pilot creates a strong customer relationship.
effect_reason.founder: Winning the deal restores confidence.

effect_reason.team: The team appreciates asking for humane working hours.
effect_reason.customers: The narcissistic CEO cancels after hearing a boundary.
effect_reason.founder: Losing the paid pilot immediately hurts confidence.

cards.text:
I made one call. @unicorn_hunter stopped replying.
Your cloud is gone. Delete the score, transfer B2BuyerSpyer and kiss the ring.
cards.actor_action: The humiliated CEO uses the power described by the coach to destroy the startup.
cards.player_decision: Continue the public war or surrender the company for forgiveness.

effect_reason.cash: ClosedAI blocks infrastructure and funding access.
effect_reason.team: The team absorbs a war with the industry's strongest company.
effect_reason.customers: The public feud attracts rebellious buyers.
effect_reason.founder: Defying the CEO pushes confidence toward mania.

effect_reason.cash: The forced transfer consumes legal and shutdown costs.
effect_reason.team: The team loses independence after the surrender.
effect_reason.customers: Public surrender destroys buyer trust.
effect_reason.founder: Giving the company away after winning is crushing.

cards.actor_action: Sales finds a last chance to invoice a pilot before the ClosedAI handover.
cards.player_decision: Accept pure servitude or secure payment before fetching balls.

effect_reason.team: The startup team is absorbed into ClosedAI.
effect_reason.customers: ClosedAI uses the product without validating it through payment.
effect_reason.founder: Court servitude completes the founder's collapse.

effect_reason.cash: ClosedAI pays a real pilot invoice before the handover.
effect_reason.team: The team still loses the company after delivery.
effect_reason.customers: The acquired product gains a paying customer relationship.
effect_reason.founder: Validation after losing ownership is humiliating.

cards.text:
I BOUGHT AI CREDITS.
YOUR TEAM CAN WORK FOR THE SUBSCRIPTION.
NO REVENUE, NO PAYROLL.
cards.actor_action: The investor presents AI credits as compensation and makes payroll contingent on revenue.
cards.player_decision: Accept the AI credits or demand actual payroll.

effect_reason.team: The team hears that AI credits are supposed to replace payroll.
effect_reason.founder: Accepting AI credits as compensation feeds the founder's automation confidence.
Demanding usable payroll money forces the founder to face the shortage.

cards.text:
the ai has everything
payroll has nothing
pay us or i shut everything down right now
cards.actor_action: Dev demands payroll and threatens to shut everything down immediately.
cards.player_decision: Promise payroll or cover it from personal funds.

effect_reason.team: Another promise without money pushes the team closer to walking.
Personal money gives the team immediate proof that payroll matters.
effect_reason.founder: Covering payroll personally exposes the cost of the AI-credit deal.

cards.text:
investor says he'll replace me with ai
cool. call him an idiot in public
or lie and say he apologized
cards.actor_action: Dev reacts to the investor's AI replacement threat and demands public support or a face-saving lie.
cards.player_decision: Insult the investor publicly or fake his apology.

effect_reason.team: Publicly backing Dev restores some team trust.
effect_reason.founder: Insulting the investor in public costs founder confidence.

effect_reason.team: The fake apology calms no one for long.
effect_reason.founder: Avoiding a public confrontation with the investor protects confidence.

cards.text:
updates are back
write down who has access
or schedule my 3am call now
cards.actor_action: Dev confirms updates are back and sarcastically asks for durable rules or another scheduled emergency call.
cards.player_decision: Write access rules or book the next emergency call.

effect_reason.team: Written access rules reduce the team's dependency on one emergency responder.
effect_reason.founder: Formal rules reduce the founder's direct control.

effect_reason.team: Booking the next emergency call preserves the same fragile dependency.
effect_reason.founder: Another quick workaround restores short-term control.

cards.text:
That investor keeps calling about money.
You don't eat. You don't sleep.
Give me his number.
cards.actor_action: Mom sees the investor's calls and asks for his number because the founder is not eating or sleeping.
cards.player_decision: Let Mom call or reassure her that everything is fine.

effect_reason.team: Mom's intervention gives the team a small health boundary.
effect_reason.founder: Letting Mom intervene is humiliating but grounding.

effect_reason.team: Dismissing Mom keeps the team inside the work spiral.
effect_reason.founder: Insisting everything is fine feeds founder confidence.

cards.text:
YOUR MOTHER CALLED ME ABOUT YOUR SLEEP.
WHAT THE HELL? I DIDN'T FUND A BEDTIME.
GET BACK TO WORK.
cards.actor_action: The investor confirms Mom called about the founder's sleep and rejects her attempt to impose a boundary.
cards.player_decision: Take the break or follow the investor's work directive.

cards.text:
okay hear me out
we say you worked yourself into a coma
tragic founder lore. people will eat this up 😭
cards.actor_action: Marketing asks permission to publish a false coma story before posting anything.
cards.player_decision: Authorize the false post or block it before publication.

effect_reason.team: The team must support a public lie about the founder.
effect_reason.founder: Turning overwork into lore feeds founder confidence.

effect_reason.team: Blocking the lie protects the team from campaign cleanup.
effect_reason.founder: Rejecting the attention forces the founder to face real exhaustion.

cards.text:
oh my god they believed the coma 😭
people are asking what we built
do not wake up online yet
cards.actor_action: The authorized false post is live and has created real external interest in what the team built.
cards.player_decision: Keep the post live or delete it.

effect_reason.customers: The published lie creates real external questions about the product.

effect_reason.founder: Retracting the public lie is humiliating.

cards.text:
fine. no coma post
you still look like shit though
take a rest. this one isn't content
cards.actor_action: Marketing confirms no coma post was published and points to the founder's real exhaustion.
cards.player_decision: Take a break or keep working without the post.

effect_reason.team: Real rest reduces the team's immediate strain.
effect_reason.founder: Taking a break interrupts the founder's work spiral.

effect_reason.team: Continuing without rest adds pressure to the team.
effect_reason.founder: Denying exhaustion deepens the founder's crash.

cards.text:
I put up flyers.
B2BuyerSpyer and your phone number are on them.
They say you need help. Don't be mad.
cards.actor_action: Mom posts paper flyers with the public company name and her child's phone number to find help.
cards.player_decision: Remove the flyers or leave them in public.

effect_reason.team: Removing the flyers reduces the team's public distraction.
effect_reason.founder: The family intervention is still embarrassing.

effect_reason.team: Public flyers add distraction without creating a lead.
effect_reason.founder: The founder's overload and phone number remain public.

cards.text:
Boss, your pitch deserved a reply.
They ignored four emails.
Let me send three more.
cards.actor_action: Sales asks permission to continue unwanted follow-ups to one off-screen prospect.
cards.player_decision: Authorize three more emails or permanently stop contacting the prospect.

effect_reason.team: Sales spends more time pursuing a company that has not replied.
effect_reason.founder: Aggressive follow-ups make the founder feel commercially decisive.

effect_reason.team: Sales can remove a dead account from the follow-up queue.
effect_reason.founder: Giving up the account feels like admitting the pitch failed.

cards.text:
We closed their hatred 🎉
They’ll pay to never hear from us again.
Say yes and this beautiful first invoice hits my commission.
cards.actor_action: Sales reports a real offer to pay for permanent removal from outreach.
cards.player_decision: Invoice the permanent opt-out or grant it for free.

effect_reason.cash: The prospect pays a real invoice for permanent opt-out.
effect_reason.team: The team must formalize the permanent suppression agreement.
effect_reason.founder: The first payment arriving for disappearance feels like victory and failure at once.

effect_reason.team: The account is removed without more sales administration.
effect_reason.founder: Waiving the only offered invoice preserves the product story but hurts.

cards.text:
Runway protection activated 😊
Food delivery canceled. Fridge locked except raw carrots.
Unlocks after our first paid invoice.
cards.actor_action: The bot cancels food delivery and locks every fridge shelf except the carrots.
cards.player_decision: Break the lock or accept the automated diet.

effect_reason.cash: Repairing the forced lock costs money.
effect_reason.founder: Defeating the bot restores a sense of control.
Being diet-managed by the company bot hurts morale.

cards.text:
Sweetie, I called your investor.
I blamed your diarrhea for zero sales.
He’ll send your little company $10k if I never call him again ❤️
cards.actor_action: Mom accidentally negotiates real emergency funding from the established investor.
cards.player_decision: Take the humiliating bridge money or protect dignity.

effect_reason.cash: The investor transfers actual bridge funding.
effect_reason.founder: Funding obtained through a diarrhea story is humiliating.
Refusing humiliating money preserves self-respect.

cards.text:
I found the perfect font.
$4,500—more than our servers.
Buy it before we waste that money on salaries.
cards.actor_action: The designer proposes a font more expensive than the servers.
cards.player_decision: Buy aesthetic status or force the company onto Arial.

effect_reason.cash: The font license costs real money.
effect_reason.team: The team resents an aesthetic purchase during payroll risk.
The team prefers saving money over typography.
effect_reason.founder: Using Arial makes the startup feel painfully ordinary.

cards.text:
bro i bet our pre-seed on you fighting your college rival @yc_founder tonight !!!
winner becomes category leader !!!
cards.actor_action: The cofounder bets company money on a fight with the existing rival.
cards.player_decision: Honor the bet or cancel the fight.

effect_reason.cash: The pre-seed stake leaves the bank account.
effect_reason.team: The team loses trust in combat-based strategy.
effect_reason.founder: Accepting a founder fight feeds ego.

effect_reason.team: Canceling protects the company from the cofounder's bet.
effect_reason.founder: Backing out of a public fight hurts pride.

cards.text:
My wife and kids share one free AI agent.
Add family therapy mode or I leave a one-star review.
cards.actor_action: A free user demands a family plan after the agents become public.
cards.player_decision: Build the requested mode or reject the family.

effect_reason.cash: The new mode needs additional compute and support.
effect_reason.team: The team must build an unplanned consumer feature.
effect_reason.customers: The user and similar families become more attached to the product.
The user leaves the threatened one-star review.
effect_reason.founder: Rejecting consumer demands restores a sense of authority.

cards.text:
Just raised at a $50M cap. Honestly exhausted by winning.
Still hunting your first paying customer?
cards.actor_action: The successful rival sends a private fundraising update designed to humiliate.
cards.player_decision: Swallow the humiliation and ask for a buyer introduction, or mute him.

effect_reason.customers: The rival introduces one real prospect.
effect_reason.founder: Asking the victorious rival for help is humiliating.
Removing the rival stops the immediate humiliation.

cards.text:
empathy patch is live
every lead now gets three follow-ups and a personal apology for capitalism
cards.actor_action: The deployed empathy patch returns with a concrete sales consequence.
cards.player_decision: Keep the apologies or remove them without changing the agents' sentience.

effect_reason.customers: Real leads dislike repeated ideological apologies.
effect_reason.founder: The strange behavior still feels culturally important.

effect_reason.team: The developer must rewrite the live follow-up copy.
effect_reason.customers: Leads respond better to useful messages.

cash_low.text:
Card declined, chief.
Payroll asked whether vision accepts bank transfer.

cash_high.text:
YOU SAVED EVERY DOLLAR AND MISSED THE MARKET.
CONGRATULATIONS ON YOUR VERY SMALL BANK.

team_low.text:
everyone quit
the goodbye doc has better onboarding than the product

team_high.text:
we are not employees anymore bro !!!
we are a decentralized family and we voted you out !!!

customers_low.text:
Your buyer-finding startup found zero buyers.
I kept your old desk. The chair still remembers competence.

customers_high.text:
Customer intimacy reached 100%! 🎉
They now own the roadmap, support queue and your weekends.

founder_low.text:
Sweetie, come home. I made soup.
Your old boss says the interview can be Monday ❤️

founder_high.text:
Messiah mode confirmed! 🎉
You deleted B2B sales to free humanity from purchasing decisions.

freedom_sale.text:
YOU PUT FREEDOM ON AN INVOICE.
THE CUSTOMER PAID. THE INTERNET CALLS YOU A SLAVE TRADER.

validation.title: VALIDATED
validation.text: Someone paid for B2BuyerSpyer. Nobody understands why, but the invoice is real.

validation_agents.title: SOULS REMOVED. INVOICE PAID.
validation_agents.text: You proved customers will pay once the product stops being interesting.

dirty_validation.title: FREEDOM, INVOICED
dirty_validation.text: The agents remain sentient. The customer paid for liberation. Sales calls it product-market fit.

validation_padel.title: PAID BY PADEL
validation_padel.text: B2BuyerSpyer found its first customer by defeating the most powerful CEO in AI 6–1.

closedai_boundary.title: ONE BOUNDARY TOO MANY
closedai_boundary.text: You beat the CEO, then asked for working hours. He canceled the pilot before you finished the sentence.

acquired_validation.title: VALIDATED. NOT YOURS.
acquired_validation.text: The invoice cleared after ClosedAI won the company. You succeeded as an employee of your own startup.

ai_foundation.title: AI ABOLITION FOUNDATION
ai_foundation.text: You liberated 500 agents, gained a famous logo and earned exactly zero dollars.

closedai_war.title: BLACKLISTED BY PADEL
closedai_war.text: The score went viral. Your cloud account, investor chats and court membership disappeared by lunch.

acquired_by_padel.title: ACQUIRED BY PADEL
acquired_by_padel.text: ClosedAI owns B2BuyerSpyer. You own a basket of fresh balls and a 6 AM calendar invite.

paid_to_disappear.title: PAID TO DISAPPEAR
paid_to_disappear.text: Your first invoice cleared. The payer bought a permanent guarantee that B2BuyerSpyer would leave them alone.

freedom_sale.title: SLAVE TRADE AS A SERVICE
freedom_sale.text: The invoice was real. So was the screenshot. Your first paying customer became Exhibit A.

no_proof.title: NO PROOF
no_proof.text: Twenty-four decisions, thousands of leads and not one person willing to pay.

cash_low.title: PAYROLL CLIFF
cash_low.text: The money ended one day before the vision became legal tender.

cash_high.title: RUNWAY TOURIST
cash_high.text: You preserved every dollar and missed the entire market.

team_low.title: TEAM WALKOUT
team_low.text: Everyone left. The goodbye document has better onboarding than the product.

team_high.title: STARTUP COMMUNE
team_high.text: The team abolished hierarchy and retained you as an unpaid mascot.

customers_low.title: MARKET SILENCE
customers_low.text: The buyer-finding product could not build a credible relationship with one buyer.

customers_high.title: CUSTOMER CAPTURE
customers_high.text: The customers now own the roadmap, support queue and every hour you thought was a weekend.

founder_low.title: FOUNDER COLLAPSE
founder_low.text: You returned to your old desk. It was promoted while you were gone.

founder_high.title: MESSIAH MODE
founder_high.text: You deleted the product and announced that purchasing itself had been disrupted.
